question: How do I book an appointment?
answer: Log in to your account click on New Appointment on the dashboard select your doctor date and time then confirm your booking.

question: How can I view my existing appointments?
answer: Go to My Appointments from the dashboard to see all your upcoming and past visits.

question: Where can I update my personal information?
answer: Click the Settings option in your profile dropdown top right to update your name phone number and address.

question: Can I change my email address?
answer: No for security reasons email addresses cannot be changed through the portal. Please contact the clinic administrator if you need an update.

question: How do I reset my password?
answer: Go to Settings then Security to update your password if you are logged in. If you forgot your password use the Forgot Password link on the login page.

question: Is there a mobile app available?
answer: Yes you can use our mobile app to manage your appointments view medical records and chat with clinic staff on the go.

question: How can I contact the clinic for support?
answer: You can use the Chat with Clinic feature on your dashboard to speak with our staff in real time.

question: How do I cancel an appointment?
answer: Navigate to My Appointments find the appointment you wish to cancel and click the Cancel button.

question: Can I see my past medical history in the app?
answer: Yes patient records and appointment history are available under the Patient Records section for authorized users.

question: Who can access my information?
answer: Your data is securely stored and only accessible by you and the authorized clinic staff Admin and Doctors.

question: What should I do if I encounter a bug in the app?
answer: Please report any technical issues through the Chat with Clinic feature or contact the administrator.

question: How do I know if my appointment is confirmed?
answer: You will receive a notification in the Notifications section of your dashboard once your appointment is approved by the staff.
